diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was made based on a combination of clinical, histopathologic, and ultrastructural findings. Clinical examination showed hyperkeratotic papules on the left lower extremity. Histopathologic study revealed compact orthokeratotic hyperkeratosis, often with areas of parakeratosis, and the stratum granulosum was absent or reduced. Ultrastructural study showed laminar hyperkeratotic lesions with persistence of desmosomes and degenerated cytoplasmic structures in the stratum corneum, along with atrophy of the epidermis and occasional Odland bodies.
